campaigns.title: Help Rajeev save centuries-old folk arts from disappearing forever and support impoverished tribal artists
campaigns.description: “Most of the artisans are unable to sell their artwork. They can't directly access the market. Their families are bearing the brunt. They can't send their children to school. They are going hungry. And the only way to help them is through proper support.”says Rajeev Ranjan, founder of our NGO partner ‘Winner With You’ which supports these artisansFor generations, tribal artisans have kept India's rich cultural heritage alive. But sadly, today many are being forced to abandon their art, as poverty tightens its grip. Without urgent help, both their livelihoods and their legacies could disappear forever.Your donation can help stop this loss. Support 300 tribal artisans with training, raw materials, and market access now. Donate generously!Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Suffering too severe to ignoreRajeev Ranjan understands struggle. Raised in remote villages under severe hardship, he worked his way through school, earned an education, and eventually built a career in Europe. But when he returned home, what he saw shook him: artisans, once proud custodians of tribal art, living in extreme poverty, their crafts devalued, their children dropping out of school, and their skills on the verge of extinction.“This tribal art form is an integral part of India's cultural heritage. Artisans have preserved these art forms for centuries,” he says. Yet, in today’s fast-paced world, their talents are going unseen, unrecognized—and unpaid.Disconnected from modern markets and unable to compete with mass production, many artisans have had no choice but to leave their craft behind. “Many of them have left their craft to work as labourers or to collect firewood from the forest,” Rajeev explains.What we’re losing isn’t just art, it’s identity, tradition, and the voices of communities that shaped India’s heritage. And once these skills are lost, they cannot be revived. This is a crisis not just of income, but of dignity. And it’s one we can still prevent together.Raw materials, tools, and support to restore hopeRajeev launched Craft Hub, an initiative of Winner With You, to give artisans a fighting chance. It provides them with skill training, improved product development, raw materials, and—most critically—direct market access.“Through Craft Hub, we can give them the support, we can give them a direct market link. We can try to teach them to make better products with their art. We can teach them how to brand and market their products.”This support not only improves the quality of their craft, but also restores their confidence and ability to earn with dignity. With it, artisans can send their children to school, return to their roots, and reclaim their rightful place in the economy.Donate now. Preserve heritage. Change lives.Winner With You is currently working to support 300 artisans with tools, training, and opportunities to thrive. But the need is urgent. “We are at a critical stage, and without your support, these artisans will continue to struggle and their art will be lost forever,” says Rajeev.“Please help them and support them so that we can preserve the cultural heritage. Your contribution will directly impact the lives of artisans and will ensure they can live their lives with dignity.”Donate today. Your generosity can protect India’s tribal art, uplift entire families, and give artisans the respect they’ve long deserved.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Winner With You in its mission to support tribal artisans who are struggling to survive and save India’s indigenous art forms from going extinct forever.

campaigns.title: Save Severe Malnourished Children of Bihar
campaigns.description:
The UN estimates that 2.1 million Indian children die every year before reaching the age of 5 – i.e. four every minute. Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) is a crucial cause of morbidity and mortality in children below five years of age in India. It is estimated that 8.33 per cent or 9,74,610 children in Bihar are severely malnourished and are at the highest risk of dying.  
 Nidan is running a Nutritional Rehabilitation Centre (NRC) in Bihar. The project helps cure severe and acute malnourished (SAM) children in the age group of 6 months to 60 months. It's a facility-based care which provides nutritious food & medication to children & mothers. They are discharged only after complete recovery. Mothers are trained on feed preparation, basic healthcare, hygiene and nutrition.  
 With NRC, Nidan aims to fight the problem of malnutrition among the children in the age group of 6 months to 60 months in the state of Bihar, India. So far Nidan has saved 1669 SAM children. The long term objective is to save the SAM children from malnutrition and gradually decrease the infant mortality rate in the targeted areas of intervention. With your generous support Nidan will be able to save SAM children. This, in return, will lead to healthy development of these children and of the whole community.

campaigns.title: Training Young Women Athletes to represent India at Olympics
campaigns.description:
Usha School of Athletics is a not-for-profit charitable sports organization focusing on uplifting the lives of underprivileged girls. We are involved solely in identifying, nurturing and grooming young budding athletes to compete at the international level. With 17 students under our tutelage, we require your support to increase the number of students to 40. 
 The girls at Usha School will be provided free boarding and accommodation facilities. Apart from helping them blossom in their sports career, we will also provide them efficient academic facilities at the nearby schools and colleges. These activities will require funds, for which we require your support. 
 Today’s budding athletes can make us proud in the future international sporting events, provided they are given the required opportunities. While the education will ensure that they stay confident, the regular training sessions would end up enhancing their athletic skills. The aim is to ensure that deserving sportsperson are given ample opportunities to showcase their talent and represent India in Olympics and other such events.

campaigns.title: Support the construction of boys hostel in Kutumb Village
campaigns.description:
About 400000 children are on the streets in India, who are vulnerable and deprived of their basic rights. Our Project Kutumb Village is an effort to provide rehabilitation services and shelter to the destitute children & children at risk. We at present have strength of 44 children (29 girls & 15 boys) living at Kutumb Village – our residential care centre. As per the government guidelines we need to accommodate the boys of age group 11-18 years in a separate building. Due to the lack of infrastructure the boys are living in the community centre building which is a temporary arrangement till we construct a boy’s hostel. This has restricted us to limit our services and intake of more boys who need shelter like this. 
 We aim to complete the construction of the boy’s hospital by 2016 end. The 1700 square feet hostel with 3 floors will accommodate the 15 boys and we also welcome other boys who plan to join us. As of now, we support the kids financially only till they turn 18 years of age but with the construction of this hostel we will increase this age to 24. 
 The hostel will serve as a house for the boys who are orphan until they are financially independent. It will provide them with a conducive environment to nurture their personalities. We will be able to enroll many more boys and help them turn an asset for the society only with your contribution.

campaigns.title: Help the victims of Bihar flood begin a new life
campaigns.description:
Floods in Bihar submerged many villages and farmlands entirely. Incessant rains destroyed poor people’s homes, and took a major toll on their livelihood resources (i.e. agricultural produce), valuables and livestock. People were just recovering from this disaster when a second wave of flooding in August hit and made the situation worse. In Bhagalpur, the water in Kharir is flowing 1.82 mtrs above the danger level. Estimates suggest that in Bhagalpur there has been a loss of more than Rs. 1000 crore due to floods which have affected a population of above 4 lakh, 4300 villages and 14 blocks have submerged in these floods. Bhagalpur is one the most severely affected districts of Bihar and has been kept in “A” category.  
 Nidan has chosen to respond in flood affected community of Bhagalpur for relief and rehabilitation work. In the initial stage, Nidan will begin with relief work. We would like to support and provide immediate relief to 500 families who are severely affected by providing them food packets, sanitation kits, tarpaulin and access to safe drinking water. The families will be selected on the basis of areas which have not received the attention of district authorities yet. The name of the family members will be listed down with support from the PRI, CBOs and community, the selected families will be provided with a coupon and upon presenting the coupon at collection centre, the relief packets will be handed over to the family. The food packet and sanitation kits will contain the following items: food packets containing rice, dal, oil, biscuit packets etc. We will also provide them sanitation kits consisting of bathing soap, tooth paste, tooth brush, comb, hand towel and sanitary pads. 
 Disasters are such moments of adversities, which cannot be averted but can be handled tactfully when fellow Indians support the sufferers. All that is required is your support for 500 flood victims. Help them recover from this adversity by providing them with hygienic food and sanitation kits.

campaigns.title: Shiksha Vistaar: Help poor children avail educational oppurtunities
campaigns.description:
Today, 8 million children in India are out of school – surrounded by poverty, illness and despair; they are fighting a daily battle for their Life- survival. The battle for survival can only be won by using quality education as a weapon. Education will lay the foundation for breaking the chains of poverty and will usher happier citizens. 
  Zonal Welfare Council has been working for welfare of underprivileged in Amtala area of South 24 Parganas, West Bengal, since 1990. Under our project, Shiksha Vistaar, we provide free pre- primary coaching to rural children. We borne the costs for books, stationary and coaching teacher fees of 30 kids who are currently enrolled with us. We also get them enrolled in government schools after their pre-primary education. This is primarily because we don’t have the infrastructure for higher classes.  
 Your contribution can help us a lot in illuminating the lives of the poor kids. We plan to construct a library and a full-fledged learning centre to ensure the quality of education gets better. We will be in a position to hire more teachers for our endeavours only with your lending hand.

campaigns.title: Support us in our Mahila Anand Project
campaigns.description:
About eight million (70%) women and adolescent girls in rural and urban areas of Northern Tamil Nadu are unskilled and disadvantaged. They are deprived of basic education and/or vocational training. This forces them to stay at home or migrate to cities in search of unskilled jobs for poor income under hazardous circumstances. This has led to serious consequences, including serious health problems. If these women and girls are provided some basic vocational training and skill development opportunities, they can become viable contributors to the family’s income. This can fulfil most of the contemporary needs (like child education) which is one of the best and immediate solutions towards personal and family welfare. 
 SHAPE offers 6-month skill training on fashion designing to enhance women empowerment. This paves their way for continued access to income generation resources for better quality of life, economic security and encourage the saving habit to proactively address women's needs. We also assist them in establishing their unit, purchase of raw material and sale of finished products. We tie them up with garment factories, wholesale merchants and State Government for supply of ready-made garments and school uniforms etc.,. 
 SHAPE has so far trained hundreds of women/girls including school drop outs in fashion designing. As a result, every beneficiary is able to earn Rs.400 to Rs.700 per day. Many of our students have set up their own business / micro-enterprise etc. This project is aimed to support the disadvantaged women community towards more self-reliant and independent personality and foster their potential and visions for a promising future. All that is required is your help!

campaigns.title: Support treatment of poor patients suffering from cancer
campaigns.description: "Cancer" The word carries not only dread, but a certain finality to it. Those suffering from it often believe they are doomed. Charotar, where the Charutar Arogya Mandal’s institutions are located, has a long history of tobacco growing. Thus, it is not surprising that the region has a high incidence of oral cancers as well.Anand and Kheda districts of Gujarat are tobacco producing areas, and as a result, tobacco is not only produced in large quantities, but is also consumed through various means in large quantities. Hence, the Mandal established the M.S. Patel Cancer Centre, a state-of-the-art facility with Radiation & Medical Oncology services in the year 2004. The Centre which was funded by Sir Dorabji Tata Trust was started with facilities for radiation and medical oncology, two Linear Accelerator, CT Simulation, 3-D Treatment Planning System, Image Guided Brachytherapy for Radiation and a full fledged Day Care Facility for the Chemotherapy. Since its inauguration by Shri Ratan Tata, the Centre has, over the last 19 years, earned the goodwill not only of the local community but also remote areas of the State.The Centre, with its modern facilities and a compassionate team of clinical and para-clinical staff as well as social workers, is proving to be one of the best options available for the treatment of cancer across the state. The M.S. Patel Cancer Centre is registered on the National Cancer Grid, which is a distinction and a recognition of the extensive work being done at the Centre. Last year, the Centre of the hospital had 37000 admissions out of which around 2500 patients required Chemotherapy cycles and around 4500 patients were put on Radiation therapy and 411 onco surgery of which around 2100 were new patients.The hospital's Cancer Centre receives patients not only Gujarat but also from States like Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan. These patients largely belong to the less privileged section of society. Needy and economically less privileged families who are barely able to make the two ends meet with their meagre incomes first draw down their own savings and liquidate assets to meet treatment costs. Borrowings and financial aid from various communities, groups and friends, family and the state would be the next resort. Despite this, many people have to discontinue treatment, unable to bear the financial burden.Charutar Arogya Mandal could treat more such cancer patients and provide more concessions to poor and less privileged families, if there was a source of fund available for concessions. We wish to reach out to more people, which is possible only with your support. For more information, please contact on rakeshp@charutarhealth.org

campaigns.title: Covid Widow Support
campaigns.description: Due to COVID-19 pandemic, many families have lost their earning members and struggling for their livelihoods.   PURE India Trust has decided to support these households and underprivileged women who lost their husband/father/son due to COVID-19 and now it would be difficult for them to earn their bread and butter. We are helping these women to become Entrepreneurs in their own villages/communities by providing them Seed funding, Skill Training, Business Mentoring, Market linkages, MSME registration & other required support. We have already empowered 300+ marginalized women to become Entrepreneurs in their communities.    We are looking to support 50 Covid Widows by establishing their own businesses so that they are financially independent.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.
